preamble: The uvular nasal [ɴ] weakens to the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ], representing a loss of nasal closure and a shift to a fricative manner of articulation at the same uvular place.

examples.note: Potential weakening of uvular nasals in rapid speech or specific phonological environments.
Nasal weakening in environments where the uvular nasal coda is followed by a fricative.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
